overview: Tensions escalate in the Iran conflict with multiple US military aircraft shot down, including an F-15E and an A-10, prompting ongoing search and rescue efforts. In US politics, Trump engages with the Supreme Court and proposes new spending and budget cuts, while China signals diplomatic support amid the Iran war. AI continues to impact industries, with advances in medical devices and film production, and Duolingo pivots toward AI-driven growth.
podcast_script: Welcome to today's briefing. The Iran conflict intensified as US military aircraft were shot down for the first time in over two decades, including an F-15E and an A-10 Thunderbolt II. Rescue operations are underway, with at least one pilot confirmed alive and in US custody. Meanwhile, Iran executed two individuals linked to opposition groups, further heightening regional tensions. In Washington, former President Trump attended Supreme Court oral arguments but left early, and he has submitted his initial proposals to Congress involving spending increases and budget cuts. China has expressed support for diplomatic solutions in the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict. On the technology front, researchers are advancing safer AI-enabled medical devices, and AI is reshaping the global film industry. Duolingo is also shifting its growth strategy to prioritize AI, balancing growth with margin trade-offs. In local news, Massachusetts comedian rescued after a crash expresses gratitude, while political figures faced boos at the Red Sox home opener. Markets showed modest gains in major US indices, while cryptocurrencies saw slight declines. That's your briefing for today.
